hook: The US is making moves on Venezuela's oil and metals, and three companies are sitting right in the middle of it.

Valera Energy [VERIFY] runs Gulf Coast refineries built for heavy crude, with over 130 billion dollars in revenue last year giving them the capital to scale fast. Gold Reserve Inc [VERIFY] is the moonshot: a sub-200-million-dollar market cap company with a 1.1 billion dollar legal award against Venezuela and a potential debt-for-mine swap on a gold and copper project worth up to 7 billion dollars [VERIFY].

hook: The SpaceX IPO is not what retail investors think it is.
beats: SpaceX is valued at 2 trillion dollars on roughly 15 billion in revenue. Amazon does over 700 billion in revenue at a similar valuation. That math is brutal.
Normal IPOs give retail about 10% of shares. Reports say SpaceX could hand retail 30% [VERIFY]. That is not generosity. They need someone to buy those shares, and they are betting on the hype.

Not financial advice, but I am not touching this one. The space sector I am watching instead is Iridium Communications [VERIFY], a global satellite network covering planes, ships, and remote systems worldwide.
